meta.title: Wave 5 — Hermes Absorption Pass
meta.description: 9 features from Nous Research Hermes Agent. Best setup/doctor UX, delegation semantics, memory composition.

props.title: Operator Shell Absorption
props.subtitle: Hermes has the best setup/doctor UX and delegation semantics in the ecosystem. 5 steal, 2 extract, 1 reject, 1 implemented.

props.content: Hermes Agent (Nous Research) is the strongest consumer-grade agent runtime in the parity set. Its setup wizard, doctor/status shell, ACP editor integration, memory-provider composition, and delegation semantics are all patterns Lev should absorb. The key insight: Hermes wins on **ceremony** — making the first 5 minutes feel professional. Lev wins on **depth** — FlowMind, DNA, graph, eval harness. Wave 5 takes Hermes ceremony into Lev depth.

props.title: 🎯 Steal → Lev Skills (5)
props.content:
**hm-01** Setup wizard → `/agent-field-kit` or new `/lev-setup`
**hm-02** Doctor/status → `/lev-align` (consumer health shell)
**hm-03** ACP editor integration → `/lev` (editor registry reference)
**hm-04** Memory-provider composition → `/lev-memory` (plugin-manager shape)
**hm-05** Delegation semantics → `/cdo-team` (isolated children, budgets)

props.title: 📋 Extract Patterns (2)
props.content:
**hm-06** Profile-scoped runtime (HERMES_HOME) → reference only (Lev uses XDG, not single-home)
**hm-08** OpenClaw migration → rollout craft pattern for `/lev-intake` (migration-as-feature)

props.title: ✅ Implemented + Rejected (2)
props.content:
**hm-09** Autowiki 3-layer (raw/wiki/schema) → already in `plugins/autowiki/`
**hm-07** Skills Hub → rejected. Lev has 170+ skills + stronger meta-workflows. Don't replace.

rows.feature: Setup wizard (hermes setup) — provider, terminal, tools, messaging sections
ACP editor integration (VS Code, Zed, JetBrains)
Delegation semantics (isolated children, budget, tool inheritance)

props.title: 🧭 → /agent-field-kit (Setup Wizard)
props.content:
**Absorb:** First-run interception flow

Hermes setup has: provider selection, terminal backend choice, tool policy config, messaging channel setup. One obvious path: install → validate env → pick provider → health check → 'you're live.'

**Section to add:** 'Consumer Setup Flow (from Hermes setup wizard)'

**Why agent-field-kit:** It already owns 'bootstrap your coding environment.' Setup wizard is the consumer-grade version.

props.title: ✅ → /lev-align (Doctor/Status)
props.content:
**Absorb:** Profile-aware runtime health check

Hermes doctor checks: provider connectivity, tool availability, terminal backend health, memory plugin status, message channel reachability. Returns actionable remediation.

**Section to add:** 'Runtime Health Shell (from Hermes doctor)'

**Why lev-align:** It already owns alignment validation. Doctor is alignment validation for the operator, not the code.

props.title: 🔌 → /lev (ACP Editor Integration)
props.content:
**Absorb:** Editor registry JSON pattern

Hermes registers with VS Code, Zed, JetBrains via JSON manifest. The pattern is: declare capabilities in a structured format, let editors discover and activate them.

**Section to add:** 'Editor Integration Registry (from Hermes ACP)'

**Note:** This is mostly a reference — Lev's primary surface is terminal, not editors. But the pattern is worth documenting for future KinglyAssistant editor integration.

props.title: 🧠 → /lev-memory (Memory Composition)
props.content:
**Absorb:** Bounded memory-provider slot model

Hermes: built-in provider + exactly 1 external plugin. This constraint prevents memory sprawl. Providers: byterover, hindsight, holographic, honcho, mem0, openviking, retaindb.

**Section to add:** 'Bounded Memory Plugin Model (from Hermes)'

**Why it matters:** Lev's core/memory/ has a rich backend registry but no composition constraint. Hermes's 'built-in + 1 external' rule is a practical design boundary.

props.title: 🤝 → /cdo-team (Delegation Semantics)
props.content:
**Absorb:** Isolated child contexts with per-child budgets

Hermes delegation: spawn isolated children, each gets own iteration budget, tool inheritance from parent, skip_memory option. Parent sets constraints, child executes autonomously within them.

**Section to add:** 'Delegation with Budget Inheritance (from Hermes)'

**Why cdo-team:** CDO-team already does multi-agent deliberation. Hermes adds the budget/isolation discipline.

props.title: hm-06: Profile-scoped runtime → reference
props.content: Hermes uses HERMES_HOME for single-home state. Lev already has XDG-disciplined paths. The pattern (profile-scoped isolation) is acknowledged; the implementation (HERMES_HOME) is rejected. Source: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md XDG compliance.

props.title: hm-07: Skills Hub → rejected
props.content: Hermes has install/browse/inspect for skills. Lev has 170+ skills, skill-builder, skill-discovery, and skills-db. Replacing Lev's skill infrastructure with Hermes's would be a downgrade.

props.title: hm-08: Migration → extract pattern
props.content: Hermes imports skills from OpenClaw upstream. The pattern (migration-as-feature) is good product thinking — document it for /lev-intake as a rollout craft reference.

props.title: 1. Edit 5 skill docs
props.content:
/agent-field-kit (setup wizard)
/lev-align (doctor/status health)
/lev (editor integration registry)
/lev-memory (bounded provider model)
/cdo-team (delegation + budget inheritance)

props.title: 2. Advance to Wave 6 OpenClaw
props.content: Smallest wave — mostly platform, not skills. Then tribunal review across all 4 waves.
